images.caption: Figure 1:AsymFLUX.2 klein generations.AsymFlow finetunes FLUX.2 klein into a pixel-space flow model, producing highly realistic images with rich visual styles and fine detail.

Figure 2:AsymFlow parameterization and recovery.(a) AsymFlow changes the standard velocity target by keeping the data term full-dimensional while replacing the noise term with its low-rank projection𝑷​ϵ{\bm{P}}\bm{\epsilon}.
(b) To recover the full-rank velocity, the low-rank component𝑷​𝒖^A{\bm{P}}\hat{{\bm{u}}}_{\mathrm{A}}is used directly, while the orthogonal component is converted using the𝒙0{\bm{x}}_{0}-to-𝒖{\bm{u}}relation in Eq. (1).

Figure 3:Orthogonal component view of AsymFlow. AsymFlow parameterization can be decomposed into a𝑷​𝒖{\bm{P}}{\bm{u}}component in the low-rank subspaceIm​(𝑷)\mathrm{Im}({\bm{P}})and an(𝑰−𝑷)​𝒙0({\bm{I}}-{\bm{P}}){\bm{x}}_{0}component in the orthogonal complementIm​(𝑰−𝑷)\mathrm{Im}({\bm{I}}-{\bm{P}}). Varying the rankrryields a parameterization family whose endpoints recover full𝒙0{\bm{x}}_{0}-prediction and full𝒖{\bm{u}}-prediction.
Figure 4:Latent-to-pixel initialization.The lifted low-rank pixel generation are semantically and structurally aligned with the decoded latent generation, leaving only a low-level gap to correct.

models.AsymFLUX.2 klein produces more realistic images with richer visual styles than prior models. More results are shown in Fig.9and10.

Figure 8:Ablation of AsymFLUX.2 klein finetuning. AsymFlow produces finer details than the DDT baseline. Variance reduction further improves details and texture but introduces excessive noise. The LPIPS perceptual correction suppresses this artifact while preserving the sharp appearance.
